notes: Runs stable but without sound + movies on a clean install. On newest Proton even connecting to multiplayer servers finally works. Just for stability I added launch option 'PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%'. However, the previous sound workaround ('protontricks 287450 directsound', 'protontricks 287450 sound=alsa') does not help anymore, I couldn't get sound to work.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes:
Here is how to get sound :
(1. Install "protontricks"
(2. Run this command "protontricks 287450 dsound directmusic"
(3. Play.
(4. .....
(5. Have phun.
Original solution comes from here : https://lutris.net/games/install/11476/view
I get the sound working, but I still get sound latency. There is lag in the sound, like when I click a menu it takes a few miliseconds for the sound to come out. But then I guess it's better than not having sound at all. Also I found some artifacts in the resource counter, and the intro movie still not playing.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes:
After applying the sound fix, the sound lag is noticeable. The Alexander the Great campaign works fine, although timers are a bit glitchy. Didn't find any visual artifacts.
Intro movie doesn't play.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: Ran "protontricks 287450 dsound directmusic" as suggested here and sound was working totally fine.

notes: Runs perfectly with full visuals. Only issue is the complete lack of sound.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: On a clean install there is no sound or movies. Sound is fixed with "protontricks 287450 directmusic" as mentioned. Strangely, the mentioned sound lag (maybe 500 ms) was almost not noticeable after I bumped up the resolution to full HD. Campaign, lobby browser, quick battle works perfectly. Tutorial still crashes on "setting up animals".
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: Multiplayer does not work. Sound needs a trick.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: Used prototricks, however, sound lag is still bad.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: After applying protontricks the sound is fine with a few miliseconds delay but there is no disturbance while playing game. Hopefully found my favorite game linux solution.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes:
`protontricks 287450 -q dsound directmusic` gets the sound working nearly perfectly, no delay or glitches in-game (a short crackle on startup is the only blemish). 

Quick battles work, the Alexander campaign works, the skill tests work, but the tutorials hang. Didn't try multiplayer.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: Even with "protontricks 287450 dsound directmusic", my sound is still crackling. I don't know why it works for the others...
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: Too many errors and problems to give a higher rating. Sound output runs only with tricks, multiplayer not possible and also otherwise very buggy.
title: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition

notes: The only problem remains in the audio. Even after installing directsound and applying the sound=alsa fix, the audio was working with some delay. Overall the game is playable, didn't notice any graphic problem as some people related.

notes: Launcher works. Gameplay also seems to work fine just as it would natively. The menu has some graphical glitches and the sound does not work without tweaks, but other than that it is very playable.
